title: WolfTpmLinuxDev::open doc comment names WOLFTPM_LINUX_DEV but transport is feature-driven
description: wolftpm-tss/src/connection.rs:133: The doc for WolfTpmLinuxDev::open says 'Calls wolfTPM2_Init with the WOLFTPM_LINUX_DEV transport. When wolfTPM is built with --enable-devtpm, ioCb is not used.' This is accurate but incomplete: the actual transport (WOLFTPM_LINUX_DEV, WOLFTPM_LINUX_DEV_AUTODETECT, or TIS) depends on which Cargo feature (linux-dev or none) was used to build wolftpm-sys. Without the linux-dev feature, wolfTPM falls back to its compile-time default (TIS/autodetect). A user reading this doc for the no-feature case won't understand what transport they're getting. The comment should describe the feature flag's effect on transport selection, mirroring the equivalent doc in wolftpm::Device::open.
